name: Sampan
prompt:
Traditional Vietnamese sampan boat. Used by civilians and VC for river/canal transport in the Mekong Delta.

Physical structure:
- Hull: flat-bottomed wooden boat, ~4m long, ~1.2m wide
  - Pointed bow, squared stern
  - Low freeboard (~0.3m sides above waterline)
  - Slight upward curve at bow (sheer)
  - Built from planks - use elongated box for hull body
  - Interior visible (flat bottom planks)
- Canopy: woven bamboo/palm leaf shade canopy over middle section
  - Arched hoops supporting a mat cover
  - 2-3 bamboo ribs/hoops
  - Woven mat/thatch covering
  - Open at front and back
- Seats: simple wooden plank seats (2), one fore, one aft
- Oar/paddle: long wooden oar/sculling oar at stern
  - Used for propulsion by sculling over stern
  - ~2m long wooden pole with flat blade
- Pole: bamboo push-pole stored alongside (optional)

Colors: Weathered wood hull (0x7B6B45), darker planks (0x5C4B30), bamboo canopy frame (0xb5a068), woven mat (0xa89050), oar wood (0x6B5B3F), water level stain (0x4a5040)
Orientation: Floating on water (hull bottom at Y=0), bow pointing +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~4m long, ~1.2m wide, ~1m tall with canopy)`,
    animations: `Gentle rocking: root node, positionTrack with subtle Y oscillation (0.02m) and rotationTrack with Z-axis tilt (+/-2 degrees), 3s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'PBR Mark II Patrol Boat',
    slug: 'pbr',
    category: 'vehicles/watercraft',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 5000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era PBR (Patrol Boat, River) Mark II. US Navy "brown water navy" patrol craft for river operations.

Physical structure (~9.5m long):
- Hull: fiberglass V-hull, fast patrol boat
  - Pointed bow, wide stern
  - Low profile, ~0.8m freeboard
  - Flat deck areas fore and aft
  - Slight flare at bow
- Pilot house: small raised cabin amidships
  - Windshield: flat glass panels (3) with wipers
  - Open top (no roof) or canvas sun cover
  - Helm/steering console inside
  - Radio antenna: whip on side
- Weapons:
  - Forward: twin M2 .50 cal on bow mount (twin barrel setup on pedestal)
  - Aft: single M2 .50 cal on stern pedestal mount
  - Amidships: M60 machine gun mount on each side
- Engine cover: raised engine housing at stern
  - Water jet intakes at stern bottom
- Ceramic armor: flat armor plates on sides of pilot house
- Flag staff: at stern for American flag
- Cleats and fittings: simplified bumpers/cleats along gunwale

Colors: Dark navy green (0x3a4a35), gray hull (0x666660), windshield glass (0x88aaaa), dark weapons (0x2a2a2a), deck (0x555550)
Orientation: Hull on water (Y=0), bow pointing +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~9.5m long, ~3.5m wide)`,
    animations: `Water rocking: gentle bob and roll, 4s cycle`
  },
  {
    name: 'Tunnel Entrance (VC)',
    slug: 'tunnel-entrance',
    category: 'structures',
    assetCategory: 'prop',
    budget: 1000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era Viet Cong tunnel entrance. Hidden entrance to the extensive Cu Chi tunnel network.

Physical structure:
- Entrance frame: rectangular hole in ground, ~0.6m x 0.5m
  - Reinforced with bamboo/wood frame around edges
  - Frame sits flush with ground surface
- Trapdoor: wooden/bamboo hinged cover
  - Flat panel matching ground level
  - Show partially open (tilted up ~45 degrees on hinge)
  - Thin construction (~0.03m thick)
  - Leaf/dirt camouflage on top surface
- Tunnel shaft: dark hole visible below trapdoor
  - Rectangular shaft going down, ~1m deep before turning
  - Dark interior (dark brown/black materials)
- Camouflage: leaves, twigs, dirt around entrance
  - Small flat irregular pieces scattered on ground near entrance
  - Ground surface is jungle floor
- Ventilation: small bamboo tube poking up nearby (optional, ~0.02m diameter, ~0.3m tall)
  - Disguised as plant stem

Colors: Bamboo frame (0xb5a068), trapdoor wood (0x6B5B3F), leaf camouflage (0x3a5a1a), dirt (0x5a4a2a), dark tunnel interior (0x1a1510), jungle floor (0x4a5a2a)
Orientation: Flush with ground at Y=0, trapdoor opens toward +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~0.6m x 0.5m entrance, ~1m deep shaft)`,
    animations: `Trapdoor: opens (rotates -90 degrees on X axis from closed) then closes over 3s cycle`
  },
];

const BATCH_4: AssetDef[] = [
  {
    name: 'F-4 Phantom II',
    slug: 'f4-phantom',
    category: 'vehicles/aircraft',
    assetCategory: 'environment',
    budget: 5000,
    prompt: `Vietnam War era McDonnell Douglas F-4E Phantom II. Primary US fighter-bomber, distinctive large twin-engine design.

Physical structure:
- Fuselage: large, long fuselage ~19m, wide rear section
  - Nose: pointed radome, slightly drooped
  - Tandem cockpit: two seats under long bubble canopy (pilot front, WSO rear)
  - Spine: raised spine along top of fuselage
  - Intake ramps: large rectangular air intakes on each side of fuselage under cockpit
- Wings: swept-back wings with distinctive upturned tips (12-degree dihedral at tips)
  - Leading edge slats
  - Trailing edge flaps
  - ~11.7m span
- Tail: distinctive low-set horizontal stabilizer with anhedral (downward angle)
  - Single vertical stabilizer, tall
- Engines: two J79 turbojets in rear fuselage
  - Two large exhaust nozzles at rear
  - Distinctive afterburner "turkey feathers"
- Landing gear: tricycle (can show retracted - gear doors closed)
- Weapons:
  - Centerline hardpoint: fuel tank or bomb
  - Wing pylons: 4 total (2 per wing), can carry bombs or rockets
  - Show 4-6 Mk 82 bombs on pylons (cylindrical with fins)

Colors: SEA camouflage - tan upper (0x8B7355), green patches (0x556B2F), light gray under (0xaaaaaa), dark engine nozzles (0x333333), canopy glass (0x6688aa), bombs OD green (0x556B2F)
Orientation: On ground or in flight pose (slight nose up), facing +Z
Scale: Real-world meters (~19m long, ~11.7m wingspan)
animations: No animation for static aircraft (or subtle engine glow pulse on exhaust)
